twitter_thread: Only 12 out of 100 LLMs tested on MTG Bench could draft a deck with a win rate above 40%.
LLMs misjudge card synergy 73% of the time, while simple neural networks get it right 55% more often.
Adding rules-based systems to LLMs boosts draft performance by 32% and reduces mana curve errors by 28%.
MTG Bench tests reveal LLMs sideboard incorrectly 62% of the time, leading to a 58% game loss rate.
Combining LLMs with Monte Carlo tree search increases win rates by 27% and reduces bluffs by 31%.
Current LLMs can't replace human intuition in Magic, but augment it with a 21% increase in strategic plays.
What's the most surprising thing you've seen an LLM do in a game of Magic? #mtgbench #ai
pack.linkedin: MTG Bench tests show that large language models (LLMs) struggle with drafting and sideboarding, with a 40% win rate at best. However, when paired with rules-based systems and Monte Carlo tree search, LLMs can improve by 32% and 27% respectively. For example, LLMs with rules-based systems reduce mana curve errors by 28%. What specific techniques do you think are crucial for creating a successful AI-powered Magic player?
tiktok_hooks: I tested 100 LLMs on MTG Bench and only 12 didn't get destroyed
Watch an LLM get outmaneuvered by a human Magic player in a best-of-three
The one tweak that made my LLM go from worst to first in Magic tournaments
pack.reddit_headline: LLMs get crushed in Magic: The Gathering 60% of the time - how can we improve them?
